description: Review and merge morning-cockpit #34 (S8 read-side: derive decided-in-flight from closes: refs). Run `pnpm test` first — this is the declared entrance check for the slice. The write-side (PRs #32, #33) is already merged; #34 completes the decision→delivery seam. Agent-eligible: no design judgment required, automated check is sufficient.

Write an ADR in core formalising the single-reconciler-writer pattern established in S4 (commit f501b25): declare that core's own reconciler is the only sanctioned writer to the northstar ledger, name the failure mode of dual writers (duplicate ledger entries), and specify the enforcement mechanism. S4's commit message documents the RCA; no durable ADR captures it. Without it, the next engineer to add a write path will repeat the same race condition.

Audit S10–S15 registration status: Friday committed S1–S9 and S16–S17 but six sequential steps are absent from the commit window. Determine whether S10–S15 were skipped intentionally, deferred, executed in a branch not yet merged, or are simply missing. The northstar P1 10→70 headline cannot be treated as complete until this is resolved. Name each step's current status and the next uncommissioned slice.

Audit the Notion relay handoff TTL for northstar confirmations: determine the maximum window between voice-confirmation and registry landing before an entry is at risk of being lost. BlogEngine #58's 17-day orphan window is the reference case. Document the safe window and whether an automated pickup exists.

Execute the core #150 → BlogEngine #57 → core #151 → oldest-first cleaner PR merge sequence. Do not batch core #150 or #151 with the cleaner PRs — they carry different review weight. Target: zero PRs older than 72 hours across all repos.

Add a post-generation check to the ojfbot publish pipeline: reject any output where the lede matches the known failure sentinel `_(Article generation` or where JSON schema parsing fails. Pipeline should halt and alert rather than publish.

Add payload-size logging to the ADR registry injection step. Log a warning when the serialized registry exceeds a configurable threshold. Use the 163-ADR truncation event as the baseline calibration point.

Document the generation-failure recovery path as a first-class runbook: what to check, in what order, with the manual-stub entry as the explicit documented fallback. One page, plain markdown, lives in the ojfbot repo.

Trigger a session in core after the OPAV S1 C3 runtime merge and inspect the telemetry log for at least one skill:acted entry — one from the CLI recorder path (explicit /validate invocation) and one from the Stop-hook path (session end). Both must appear for C3 to close. If the Stop-hook path is silent, check the hook script's fnm default version pin — that's the first place to look after 1074243. This is the prerequisite before the suggestion-funnel numbers are trustworthy.

Merge the daily-cleaner PR queue in this sequence: (1) core #150 first — fleet-wide behavioral change, adds frame-standup to user-scope baseline, review carefully; (2) BlogEngine #57 — 63% token reduction in always-loaded CLAUDE.md, no blocker; (3) core #151 — ADR-0081 tracker sync, merge after #57; (4) batch the remaining cleaner PRs oldest-first: core #152, #153, #159, #162, landing #25, daily-logger #215. Do not batch #151 with the cleaner PRs — it carries different review weight. Target: zero open daily-cleaner PRs and the ADR-0081 chain closed.

Run the f1-pit-wall corpus eval harness (committed in 1a59ff0) against POST /api/route with at least 10 labelled queries. Each label should specify the expected intent type (discriminated union variant) and expected slot values. Record the deterministic-first coverage rate and LLM fallback accuracy. This is the first-ever validation run — there is no baseline yet. The harness must pass before the C1 jargon-detection gate (ADR-0003) can be scoped.

Review and merge Core #150 (add frame-standup to user-scope baseline install — one-file change to install-agents.sh and the managed user CLAUDE.md template) and Core #147 (vault canvas node sizing — one-line schema addition). Both are low-risk, have no stated blockers, and were skipped Thursday for lack of bandwidth. Merge #150 first; it is a fleet-wide behavioral change. Merge #147 second.

Add a test for the pipeline-alert issue-ops layer (1d21c5d): simulate a workflow failure in dry-run mode and confirm (a) a GitHub issue is opened and assigned, (b) the push notification fires via the GitHub App, and (c) running /disable sets the suppression variable and prevents a second issue from being opened on the next failure. Then confirm the variable can be manually reset to restore alerting. No test currently covers the suppression path.

Merge daily-logger #210 (register f1-pit-wall, f1-substrate, lofi-beaver, golf-platform-scripts in the daily sweep). Four repos born during the outage window are producing commits that do not appear in daily articles. This is a live data-integrity gap. Merge this before #209.

Merge daily-logger #209 (backfill digest for 2026-05-19→06-09) after #210 lands. The backfill is reconstructed from git history and is independently mergeable, but sequencing it after #210 ensures the four dark repos are included. Done when the backfill articles are generated and the gap in the dev log is closed.

Enable the defuddle shadow-compare step in selfco-box and run five real URL ingests. Review the comparison log in the selfco-box run directory. Promotion criterion: readability extraction is visibly cleaner than raw on noisy URL sources and does not drop source:: frontmatter or inline tags. Record the outcome as a note in the vault. Done when you have a written go/no-go on promoting the step to opt-in default.

Run semantic-suggest.py against the live selfco vault. Count how many suggested cross-cluster links survive manual review as genuinely non-obvious connections. Record the raw precision rate. Decision rule: below ~30%, tune threshold or model before promoting; above ~60%, candidate for opt-in default. This baseline measurement is the exit condition ADR-0091 requires.

Review and merge ADR-0079 (core #118, vault page-lifecycle policy). Filed 2026-05-16, currently Proposed. The /vault skill shipped Sunday has no staleness governance until this advances to Accepted — sessions reading from the vault via the planned obsidian-mcp transport will produce confidently-wrong answers from stale pages with no user-visible signal. This is the prerequisite gate for the transport upgrade. Accept or request changes; do not leave it in Proposed.

Review ADR-0080 (core #121, vault staleness scanner). Depends on ADR-0079 (#118) — do not merge before #118 is Accepted. Opened 2026-05-18. Confirm the implementation decisions in #121 are consistent with the page-lifecycle policy in #118. Specifically: #121 refines mechanism (2) from #118; verify the scanner behavior aligns with whatever lifecycle boundaries ADR-0079 establishes.

Audit the Sunday suggestion log: 5 suggestions offered, 0 followed, 0 sessions with quality skill coverage. Thirteenth consecutive day of 0% follow rate. Locate the raw suggestion log entries for the Sunday session window. Classify at least 5 entries by source: realtime suggestion (offered during an active session) vs. PR-comment-marker (comment-marker on a PR counted as a suggestion credit). Produce a single written disposition — instrumentation failure or agent non-compliance — and name the remediation path. This is the last reliable classification window; session context that disambiguates source attribution degrades beyond ~two weeks. Do not defer again.

Review and merge core #117 (Pocock skill conventions: /prototype, /caveman, /zoom-out, writing pipeline, /git-guardrails). Open six days, no conflicts or CI failures noted. Solo project — the review gate is a habit gap, not a technical one. Prerequisite for expanding the active skill surface in all subsequent sessions. Do this first.

Write a CLAUDE.md entry in workstation-yuri (not a full ADR — that's for core) codifying the boundary rule established by commit 1029c2b: 'Machine-specific, OS-specific, or hardware-tied concerns belong in workstation-yuri; portable skill catalog logic stays in core.' Also include the three-question test: Is it OS-specific? Is it tied to a display profile or hardware layout? Is it named after or configured for a single machine? Commit this before the next session adds something ambiguous.

Set a calendar date for the ADR-0068 compliance audit in the next session. The audit has been open since 2026-05-05 (twelve days). Investigation actions act-2026-05-05-investigate-f18cb5 and act-2026-05-10-investigate-d5cfb2 remain open. Until the audit runs, the nine-session 0% telemetry streak is uninterpretable — it cannot be distinguished from instrumentation failure. This needs a date, not another deferred action.

Open frame-dev.sh and shell's remoteBase map. Grep for :3037 to confirm it is unoccupied. Reassign asset-foundry from :3035 to :3037. Commit. Rebase Shell PR #76 (register asset-foundry MF remote). Closes act-2026-04-30-roadmap-640008, act-2026-05-05-validate-0baa3c, and act-2026-05-10-fix-a78f2f. Estimated time under 15 minutes. This is the first task in the next commit window.

Pull raw session suggestion logs from today's three sessions across core, core-library, selfco, and related repos. Classify at least 5 suggestion entries by source (real-time surface vs. PR comment marker). Write one finding paragraph: which hypothesis the classification supports (instrumentation inflation or ADR-0068 non-compliance) and whether enforcement should proceed or instrumentation needs repair first. Expected time: 20-40 minutes. Output lands in the ADR-0068 audit record.

Merge all seven open daily-cleaner PRs across four repos: beaverGame #42, core-reader #29, landing #23, daily-logger #191, asset-foundry #30, daily-logger #195, daily-logger #198. Before merging core-reader #29, confirm no conflict with in-flight feature branches — it has been open six days in an active repo. Target: queue resets to zero. Acceptable steady-state is zero PRs older than 72 hours.

Run the ADR-0068 telemetry audit now — this action has been open for five days and has no technical blocker. Grep the suggestion log for Saturday's four sessions across lofi-beaver, asset-foundry, core-library, and ojfbot. Classify each of the 4 suggestions by source: realtime vs pr-comment-marker. Determine whether the 0% follow rate reflects comment-marker inflation (instrumentation problem) or genuine agent non-compliance (ADR-0068 enforcement problem). Output: a classification table and a disposition — either 'telemetry fix needed' or 'agent behavior correction needed.' This is the prerequisite before ADR-0068 is enforceable.

Advance ADR-0012 or formally park it — 'Proposed with no named reviewer and no acceptance criteria' is a stale ADR by another name. Either: (a) write the acceptance criteria (interface contract schema between ResearchCurator and generation agents, sequencing rationale for introducing upstream agent before execution layer is visible in dev), assign a reviewer, and set a review window; or (b) explicitly park it with a stated reason and a condition under which it would be revisited. Do not leave it in the current state.

Resolve the :3035 port collision in frame-dev.sh and the shell remoteBase map. Steps: (1) grep the full shell config for ':3037' to confirm it's unoccupied, (2) reassign asset-foundry from :3035 to :3037 in both files, (3) commit, (4) rebase the shell PR (act-2026-04-29-pr-review-e613c0) against main and merge. Closes act-2026-04-30-roadmap-640008 and act-2026-05-05-validate-0baa3c.

Confirm the article-quality verification layer (5ab335f) catches a known-bad claim. Craft a test prompt referencing a non-existent ADR number, run it through the pre-generation step, and verify the layer produces a flagged placeholder rather than a confident assertion. If the layer passes the bad claim, Wednesday's article output cannot be treated as verified.

Audit the 2026-05-06 suggestion log to separate real-time credits from comment-marker credits. Run: grep '2026-05-06' ~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl. Classify each entry by source field (realtime vs pr-comment). Recompute the 3-suggested/0-followed funnel against real-time credits only. Determine whether the 0% follow rate is structurally guaranteed by comment-marker inflation or reflects actual non-compliance. This audit is the prerequisite before ADR-0068 can be treated as enforceable.

File BG-001 (HDRI), BG-003 (snap CI), BG-005 (Poisson scatter), and BG-015 (material verification) as tracked issues on the Babylon-era beaverGame codebase. The Babylon migration is complete and these Phase 1 items have no filed issue state. Assign an owner to each before the next standup.

Close or re-scope beaverGame #29 (Babylon migration umbrella). All sprint PRs (#32, #37, #38, #39) are merged — post a completion summary on the issue and explicitly re-file BG-003 (snap CI), BG-005 (Poisson scatter), BG-001 (HDRI), BG-015 (material verification) as Phase 1 tracking items. This is the 10-minute action that makes 'migration complete' mean something on the roadmap.

Resolve the :3035 port collision and run the first end-to-end test of the asset-foundry Frame remote in the same session. Open frame-dev.sh and the shell remoteBase map, assign gcgcca a distinct port, update both files in a single commit (closes act-2026-04-30-roadmap-640008), then run frame-dev.sh and confirm asset-foundry loads at :3035 without a federation error.

Diagnose the 46-suggested / 0-followed funnel from Monday's sessions before ADR-0068 is treated as enforceable. Read ~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl and classify at least 5 suggestion entries by reason-for-ignore: wrong context, wrong moment in session flow, genuinely ignorable, or agent non-compliance. Separately, confirm whether PR-comment marker credits are being counted as real-time skill invocations in the audit — if they are, the telemetry model needs correction before the compliance rule compounds further on synthetic data.

Add a precision-over-reach rule to the daily-logger style guardrails: when describing partial rollouts or adoption, state the explicit count (e.g. "across 6 repos") rather than using totalising language like 'fleet-wide', 'all', or 'every' unless 100% coverage is verified. This prevents future articles from overstating scope the way this article's original summary did.

Audit the :3035 port collision between asset-foundry and gcgcca. Open frame-dev.sh and shell's remoteBase map side by side, assign a distinct port to each remote, and update both files in a single PR. Shell #76 must not merge until this is resolved — merging it as-is bakes the collision into the host config. The fix is mechanical: pick a free port for asset-foundry (e.g. :3036), update frame-dev.sh and remoteBase in one commit, then amend #76 or open a follow-on PR.

Disposition beaverGame #27 (Three.js playability pass). Open the PR and decide: merge-as-spec or close-as-superseded. If closing, extract the heightAt(x,z) bilinear-blend fix into #28's behavioral-spec checklist before deleting the branch — that logic is the terrain-sampling spec the Babylon migration must satisfy. Leave a comment in #28 linking to the decision. This takes under 15 minutes and is the single action that unblocks Babylon bootstrap.

Begin the Pocock skills adoption triage with core #81 only. #81 (foundation: /grill-with-docs, CONTEXT.md, agent defaults) is the prerequisite for all subsequent phases (#82–#85). Review and merge #81 before opening #82 for review. Merging out of order produces skill catalog phase-registry inconsistencies where later commands reference skill entries that do not yet exist. Do not batch-review all five — proceed one PR at a time in phase order.

Merge core #94 (game-engine architecture spike — Babylon.js recommended). This is the decision record that justifies discarding beaverGame's entire Three.js codebase. Draft PR #28 is already open; without #94 merged, the rationale exists only in a PR description and will be re-litigated in future sessions.

Name a disposition for beaverGame #27 (Three.js playability pass) before #28 advances past the Babylon bootstrap. The heightAt(x,z) bilinear-blend fix is a correctness issue — leaving #27 open creates ambiguity about what the snap-rig oracle actually certifies. Merge it as spec, or close it as superseded. Do not leave it in an undecided state.

Merge shell #76 (register asset_foundry MF remote at :3035). One-line host config addition, zero risk surface, prerequisite for the asset-foundry Frame MF front door being visible in the dev environment. Can close in under ten minutes and unblocks the asset-foundry platform roadmap.

Write Vitest tests for beaverGame's load-glb.ts validation tripwire covering all four rejection paths: missing validation.json, rejected status field, malformed JSON, and schema mismatch. The tripwire is the gate that prevents a malformed or unvalidated .glb from being loaded into the scene — if it passes something it shouldn't, a corrupted or oversized asset silently enters the render pipeline. This is BG-004 and the highest-leverage untested code in the loader. The four paths map directly to the four branches in the validation check function.

Add the snap-and-diff CI step to beaverGame's .github/workflows/ci.yml. The snap step runs pnpm tsx scripts/snap.ts to render a headless screenshot of the full scene in its default initial state (terrain loaded, no player actions taken), uploads the result as a workflow artifact, and diffs it against the committed baseline at tests/baselines/scene-snap.png using SSIM ≥ 0.98 as the pass threshold. On the first run, if no baseline exists, the step should write the baseline and pass — subsequent runs enforce the diff. This is BG-003 and the prerequisite for visual regression as a CI gate.

Verify the asset-foundry → beaverGame asset sync end-to-end: with both repos checked out as siblings (../beaverGame and ../asset-foundry), run pnpm gen-asset birch_sapling in asset-foundry and confirm that birch_sapling.glb and birch_sapling.validation.json land in beaverGame/public/assets/. Document the expected sibling-directory layout in asset-foundry's CLAUDE.md. This is the manual precursor to AF-013 CI automation and currently the highest-risk undocumented path in the ecosystem — the Phase 0 spike was developed in isolation and this sync has never been verified against a real beaverGame checkout.

Verify conflict state of daily-logger daily-cleaner PRs #155, #157, #159, #161 (opened April 14–17) before touching #163–#169. Open each PR in the GitHub UI, check for conflict markers, and manually resolve any conflicts on the affected branches. Once the oldest four are clean, run act-2026-04-21-sweep-914db8 to batch-merge all eight. Do not invoke the batch-merge action until conflict state is verified — the queue is 8 PRs deep and a single conflicted branch will block the entire batch.

Audit the approximately 16 open actions generated before commit `9c17d7f` for ID correctness: re-run the content hash (command + description + repo + date, whitespace-normalized) against each stored ID. Any mismatch means the action will not close in the next closure pipeline run. Remediate by re-generating the ID and updating the action file, or manually verifying the action is still open and re-writing with the correct hash. Complete before the next sprint closure run.

Review purefoy #39 (GPU transcription observability / ProgressReporter). Validate the 8-stage progress.json schema, confirm ProgressReporter does not block the transcription hot path, and merge. PR has been open since at least 2026-04-11 and is merge-pending-review with no named reviewer.

Run /validate on shell's /api/beads filesystem fallback to unblock Gas Town Sprint 1. This is not blocked by MrPlug #28 and has no active owner. It is the first gate before the core-reader bead consumption scaffold can proceed.

Verify shell's Dolt-first /api/beads aggregation falls back correctly to filesystem bead files when Dolt is unavailable. Start the shell dev server, hit the endpoint, confirm it returns beads from at least two sub-apps using the filesystem path at {repo}/.beads/{session-id}/{event-type}.json, and document the bead file location convention in shell's README. This is a prerequisite for the core-reader scaffold action.

Write the fleet-wide GET /api/beads ADR. ADR-0016 already exists and governs the per-app projection pattern. This new ADR documents the fleet extension: the FrameBeadLike type (defined in core/packages/types/src/bead.ts), the app-specific prefix convention (pure-, blog-, trip-, seh-, lean-), the empty-return-until-storage pattern, the shell Mayor aggregation strategy, and the Dolt migration path for filesystem beads. Eight repos implement this contract today without a durable decision record.

Scaffold the bead consumption view in core-reader after /validate confirms the filesystem fallback path works. Read aggregated beads from shell's /api/beads endpoint, render a raw timeline list in one of core-reader's existing tabs. Use the filesystem-fallback path — do not wait for Dolt. This is the first visible Gas Town UI and the proof that the Mayor aggregation layer works end-to-end.

Confirm the Vite alias for @ojfbot/frame-ui-components resolves from source correctly during local development after the npm migration — run the shell dev server, import a component from frame-ui-components, and verify hot-reload reflects source edits without a publish step.

Run the Storybook composition dashboard (shell #66) after the npm migration to confirm all fleet repos' component stories still resolve correctly — the file: → npm change may break story resolution if the Storybook config references local paths.

cv-builder BioDashboard is now 373 lines after the decomposition — still above the container-presenter 100-line orchestrator target. Identify the next extraction candidates and open a follow-up decomposition PR.

Add an explicit wait-for-sentinel pattern to the Playwright screenshot test and confirm it survives a cold Module Federation boot — i.e., clear the Vite cache and re-run the capture script end-to-end to verify the fix holds under slow-network conditions.

Write ADR for the Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript codegen pattern established in gcgcca PRs #1 and #2. Document the invocation (python scripts/generate_schema.py, venv required, output to src/generated/types.ts), the known failure mode (custom validators and Union discriminators may not map cleanly to OpenAPI), and the purefoy CLI+UI reference. This pattern will apply to every future Python-backed Frame sub-app.

Register gcgcca as a live Module Federation remote in the shell host config at :3035. Wire it into frame-dev.sh and confirm hot-reload works in the dev environment. Type bridge is complete, MF scaffold exists — this is the milestone that makes gcgcca visible inside the Frame shell for the first time.
Scope the Hailstone migration. Define what moves, what gets rebuilt, and the target architecture inside the portfolio. Produce a short migration plan before any code is written.
